_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
min_doctor_qualification: MD / DNB/equivalent (in General medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_indications: Whites of the eyes, known as icterus
Painful swelling of the hands and feet
Pain in the chest, back, arms, or legs

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers with treatment chart

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Do the clinical notes and Electrophoresis report confirm sickle cell anemia?

cpd_claim.q: Were the ICPs with detailed treatment chart submitted?
Is the post procedure High-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) report submitted?
Was the detailed discharge summary with follow-up date mentioned?

common_queries: Clinical notes and Electrophoresis report do not confirm sickle cell anemia.
Clinical notes are missing, incomplete, or do not include evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, or planned line of treatment at pre-authorization.
Electrophoresis report is missing at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers (ICPs) with treatment chart are missing or incomplete at claim submission.
High-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) report is missing at claim submission.
Detailed discharge summary is missing or does not include follow-up date at claim submission.

clinical_key_pointers: Sickle cell Anemia is a genetic disorder that results in the formation of sickled red blood cells (RBCs). The process is further aggravated by abnormal interactions of these RBCs with leukocytes, platelets, vascular endothelium, and clotting factors thus causing acute and chronic complications.

Complications of Sickle cell Anemia include Stroke, Acute chest syndrome, Kidney disease, and Venous Thromboembolism, as well as an extensive range of issues such as psychosocial problems, infections, musculoskeletal complications, cerebrovascular events, cardiac complications, hepatobiliary complications, pulmonary complications, renal complications, ocular complications, pregnancy-related complications, and multiorgan failure.
